title: Collective dynamics in a one-dimensional Heisenberg ferromagnetic spin chain
authors: R. Arun, M. Lakshmanan, Avadh Saxena
abstract: We investigate the different oscillatory modes, namely, complete synchronization, inphase synchronization, antiphase synchronization and desynchronization in a one-dimensional anisotropic Heisenberg ferromagnetic spin chain consisting of a large number of spins. By solving the associated Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert-Slonczewski equation for the spins we show the simultaneous existence of the above mentioned oscillatory modes in the spins. We observe that when the number of the spins is large the synchronization is lost between the spins; however, we identify that the field-like torque is able to induce synchronous oscillations of the spins in the chain again. We also confirm the agreement of the numerically obtained values of the frequency of the inphase synchronized oscillations with the analytically obtained values.
summary: This paper analyzes the complex collective dynamics, including various synchronization modes, within a 1D anisotropic Heisenberg ferromagnetic spin chain. By solving the LLGS equation, the authors demonstrate that while large chains tend toward desynchronization, applying a field-like torque can successfully restore coherent inphase or complete synchronization. The findings confirm the simultaneous existence of multiple oscillatory states depending on external driving fields.
main_problem: The study investigates different oscillatory modes, including synchronization states (complete, inphase, antiphase), within a one-dimensional anisotropic Heisenberg ferromagnetic spin chain.
main_result: The field-like torque is shown to be capable of restoring synchronization that is otherwise lost as the number of spins increases. The system exhibits multiple coexisting dynamic modes depending on applied fields.
method: The dynamics are analyzed by solving the Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert-Slonczewski equation numerically and comparing results with analytical derivations for oscillation frequencies.
model_or_system: The model is a one-dimensional anisotropic Heisenberg ferromagnetic spin chain, whose evolution is governed by the LLGS equation. The Hamiltonian includes terms related to nearest-neighbor interactions ($A, B, C$) and external fields.
key_observables: Oscillatory modes (complete synchronization, inphase/antiphase synchronization, desynchronization), standard deviation ($\sigma$), and oscillation frequency ($f$).
important_parameters: The system is studied by varying the number of spins ($N$) and applying specific field-like torques ($ eta$, $H_x, H_y, H_z$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on solving the LLGS equation and making approximations to derive analytical expressions for synchronized oscillation frequencies.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate time evolution of spin components ($S_x$), the loss/restoration of synchronization quantified by $\sigma$, and phase difference plots confirming various synchronization states (inphase, antiphase).
paper_structure: The paper progresses from defining the physical model and governing equation to numerically demonstrating the existence and dependence of different oscillatory modes on system size and external torques, culminating in analytical validation.
why_interesting: This work explores collective dynamics and emergent order (synchronization) in a quantum spin chain using classical/semi-classical equations of motion. The restoration of coherence via specific field terms is relevant to understanding dissipation and long-range correlations in many-body systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
